add_params.name: Action
add_params.description: Dispatch action. Formatter sends 'AddRoleAdvanced' when advancedMode=true, or omits Action (defaults to 'AddRoleSimple') when false. PS1 defaults to 'AddRoleSimple' if not provided. Valid: AddRoleSimple, AddRoleAdvanced, ListGroups.

add_params.name: gdapRoles
add_params.description: Array of {label, value} role objects. Used in AddRoleSimple mode. PS1 reads $Request.Body.gdapRoles.

add_params.name: customSuffix
add_params.description: Optional group name suffix. Used in AddRoleSimple mode.

add_params.name: templateId
add_params.description: If set, adds role mappings to this GDAP template. Used in AddRoleSimple mode.

add_params.name: Mappings
add_params.description: Array of {groupName, groupId, roleName, roleDefinitionId} objects. Used in AddRoleAdvanced mode. PS1 reads $Request.Body.mappings (lowercase). Frontend sends as 'Mappings' (capital M); PS1 property access is case-insensitive.

notes: customDataformatter has two branches: (1) If advancedMode=true → sends {Action:'AddRoleAdvanced', Mappings: advancedMappings} where advancedMappings is component state (not form values). (2) If advancedMode=false → passes through all form values unchanged (spreads the values object). In the simple mode, form fields gdapRoles, customSuffix, templateId reach the API directly. PS1 reads $Request.Body.mappings (note: formatter sends 'Mappings', PS1 reads via 'mappings' — case-insensitive match in PS).
x_cipp_warnings: advancedMappings state is held in the component, not in RHF form values. In AddRoleAdvanced mode, only Action and Mappings are sent — all form field values are discarded.
